purpose: Phase implementation-group verdicts generated after grouped implementation batches. Use this as a bounded group-repair queue before the normal phase coverage gate.

repairPolicy.blockedMeans: explicit blocker/fallback decision exists; do not loop on this group before phase gate
repairPolicy.completeMeans: linked source/platform authority rows have enough behavior, parity, endpoint, and evidence data

requiredBehaviors: `Profile.kt` only renders the packaged profile avatar with static user text; it does not open an image viewer, decode bitmaps, inspect EXIF metadata, or support zoom/preview gestures.

`Profile.kt` renders a back command, static profile action cards, and a logout button. The back arrow calls `navController.navigateUp()`, the action cards keep placeholder click handlers, and logout clears the start destination stack before routing to onboa...

`Profile.kt` composes against `ProfileViewModel` callbacks and navigates immediately from user actions, while keeping the visible profile identity and placeholder cards static until logout is requested.
`Profile.kt` uses only packaged resources, local click handlers, and navigation callbacks; it does not request runtime permissions or access protected Android APIs.
`ProfileViewModel.onEvent` propagates the logout action by toggling login state and then invoking the navigation callback, so the profile screen reacts immediately to the logout event.
`ProfileViewModel.onEvent` only toggles persisted login state and invokes the logout callback; it does not request permissions or call protected APIs.
`ProfileViewModel` has no independent load/resume teardown path; its lifecycle responsibility is limited to servicing the logout event while the profile screen is active.
`ProfileEvent.kt` defines only the `PerformLogout` callback payload and does not represent permission requests, results, or protected API access.
`ProfileCard.kt` is a clickable text-and-divider row and does not request permissions or access protected APIs.
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.
Referenced target tests need to be covered by the latest accepted test gate or explicit verificationEvidence.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone
